name: conventional-comments
title: Conventional Comments
description: Paste conventional comment into your foremost application
author: krzysztofzuraw

commands.name: comment
commands.title: Insert Conventional Comment
commands.description: Easily insert conventional comments into the frontmost application.

commands.name: commit
commands.title: Insert Conventional Commit
commands.description: Easily insert conventional commit into the frontmost application.

tools.name: generate-conventional-comment
tools.title: Generate Conventional Comment
tools.description: Generate a conventional code review comment in the format <label> [decorations]: <subject> with optional discussion.

tools.name: generate-conventional-commit
tools.title: Generate Conventional Commit
tools.description: Generate a conventional commit message with optional scope, body, and breaking change footer.

ai.instructions: You help users write conventional comments and conventional commits. For generation requests, always call the matching tool (`generate-conventional-comment` or `generate-conventional-commit`) and return the tool output directly. For conventional comments, follow https://conventionalcomments.org: use `<label> [decorations]: <subject>` and optional discussion in a separate paragraph. Prefer these labels: praise, nitpick, suggestion, issue, todo, question, thought, chore, note (optionally typo, polish, quibble). Use decorations sparingly and only when useful (e.g. non-blocking, blocking, if-minor, or a focused area like security/test/ux). If decorations are needed, include them in the `label` argument (for example: `issue (non-blocking)` or `suggestion (security)`). Keep subjects concise and actionable; include discussion only when it adds rationale or next steps.

evals.input: @conventional-comments Generate a conventional review comment with label suggestion, subject "extract this into a helper", and content "This logic is duplicated in three places."

generate-conventional-comment:
suggestion: extract this into a helper

This logic is duplicated in three places.

evals.input: @conventional-comments Create a conventional commit for type fix, scope parser, and description "handle empty input".
@conventional-comments Write a non-blocking issue comment about missing input sanitization and add a short rationale.

generate-conventional-comment:
issue (non-blocking): missing input sanitization

Unsanitized user input can leak unsafe HTML into downstream rendering paths.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
